competency_groups.title: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structural and functional aspects of flowering plant reproduction, including gametophyte development, pollination mechanisms, double fertilization, and special reproductive modes.
competencies: Analyze flower structure and the developmental processes of male and female gametophytes.

competency_groups.title: Human Reproduction
competency_groups.description: Exploring the anatomy and physiology of human reproductive systems, gametogenesis, hormonal regulation of the menstrual cycle, fertilization, and post-fertilization events.
competencies: Evaluate the microscopic anatomy of male and female reproductive organs and their functional roles.

competency_groups.title: Reproductive Health
competency_groups.description: Assessing the importance of reproductive health, methods of contraception, prevention and management of STDs, MTP, assisted reproductive technologies, and genetic screening.
competencies: Critique various birth control methods and their implications for public health.

competency_groups.title: Principles of Inheritance and Variation
competency_groups.description: Investigating the laws of inheritance, deviations from Mendelian genetics, chromosomal theory, sex determination mechanisms, and genetic disorders.
competencies: Apply principles of Mendelian inheritance to analyze complex traits including incomplete dominance and co-dominance.

competency_groups.title: Molecular Basis of Inheritance
competency_groups.description: Examining the molecular nature of genetic material, DNA structure, replication mechanisms, gene expression regulation via operons, and applications in genomics.
competencies: Explain the Central Dogma and its role in converting genetic information into functional proteins.
